doctors.bioFr: Approche intégrative TCC et pleine conscience. Focus sur l’anxiété et les transitions de vie.
doctors.bioEn: Integrative CBT and mindfulness. Focus on anxiety and life transitions.
doctors.bioEs: TCC integrativa y mindfulness. Ansiedad y transiciones vitales.
doctors.illustrationNote: Semi-flat portrait, soft teal and cream palette, warm smile — calm professionalism.

doctors.bioFr: Soutien aux familles et aux jeunes; perspective systémique.
doctors.bioEs: Apoyo a familias y jóvenes; enfoque sistémico.
doctors.illustrationNote: Pastel greens, seated figure with plant motif — inclusive silhouette.

doctors.bioFr: Affirmatif 2SLGBTQ+; thérapie axée sur les forces et la résilience.
doctors.illustrationNote: Soft lavender and sky blue, abstract path motif — recovery-oriented.

services.bodyFr: Séances impliquant plusieurs membres de la famille.
services.bodyEs: Sesiones con varios miembros de la familia.

services.bodyFr: Bilan orienté diagnostic ou orientation de services.

testimonials.quoteFr: Le parcours public doit rendre les étapes compréhensibles avant la création de compte.
testimonials.quoteEn: The public pathway should make each step understandable before account creation.
testimonials.quoteEs: El flujo público debe hacer comprensible cada paso antes de crear una cuenta.

testimonials.quoteFr: La prise de rendez-vous doit réduire l’incertitude : motif, date, horaire, puis confirmation.
testimonials.quoteEn: Booking should reduce uncertainty: reason, date, time, then confirmation.
testimonials.quoteEs: La reserva debe reducir incertidumbre: motivo, fecha, horario y confirmación.

testimonials.quoteFr: Le contenu doit rester cohérent en français, anglais et espagnol.
testimonials.quoteEn: Content should remain coherent across French, English, and Spanish.
testimonials.quoteEs: El contenido debe mantenerse coherente en francés, inglés y español.

lead.fr: Aperçu public d’une plateforme de prise de rendez-vous, d’orientation et de coordination pour services psychologiques virtuels à Montréal. Ne traite pas les urgences.
lead.en: Public preview of a booking, intake, and coordination platform for virtual psychology services in Montreal. Not for emergencies.
lead.es: Vista pública de una plataforma de reserva, orientación y coordinación para servicios psicológicos virtuales en Montreal. No atiende emergencias.

title.fr: PsyNova — clinique virtuelle et plateforme d’accès
title.en: PsyNova — virtual clinic and care-access platform
title.es: PsyNova — clínica virtual y plataforma de acceso

blurb.fr: Catégories de services reliées au parcours de réservation.
blurb.en: Service categories connected to the booking path.
blurb.es: Categorías de servicios conectadas al flujo de reserva.
Perfiles, roles y áreas de acompañamiento.

blurb.en: Short posts citing WHO, NIH/NIMH, CDC, NHLBI, and related agencies.
blurb.es: Textos breves con citas a OMS, NIH/NIMH, CDC, NHLBI, etc.
blurb.fr: Articles courts citant OMS, NIH/NIMH, CDC, NHLBI, etc.

blogPosts.titleEs: Recursos y líneas de crisis — CDC
blogPosts.excerptFr: Points saillants des pages CDC sur l’aide immédiate (anglais original).
blogPosts.excerptEn: Quoted key points covering hotlines from CDC’s Mental Health Resources page.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
If you need help now, call a crisis hotline to get immediate emergency counseling.

Source : Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Mental Health Resources, cdc.gov, consulté mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
U.S. federal pages cluster national hotlines alongside tips for locating ongoing care.

If you need help now, call a crisis hotline to get immediate emergency counseling.

Source: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (Mental Health Resources). Accessed May 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
If you need help now, call a crisis hotline to get immediate emergency counseling.

Fuente: CDC (cdc.gov).

blogPosts.excerptFr: Présentation officielle gratuite disponible jour et nuit.
blogPosts.excerptEn: Quoted description from the federally supported 988lifeline.org site.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
Through the 988 Lifeline, you have access to free, quality, one-on-one assistance.

Source : 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline. Service américain gratuit 24 h/24 — consulté mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Crisis hotlines stabilize immediate risk until local emergency responders or outpatient teams can intervene. The U.S. 988 Lifeline routes texts and calls nationally.

Through the 988 Lifeline, you have access to free, quality, one-on-one assistance.

Source: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline. Accessed May 2026. Not affiliated with PsyNova; verify instructions on the linked site.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Through the 988 Lifeline, you have access to free, quality, one-on-one assistance.

Fuente: 988 Lifeline (EE. UU.).

blogPosts.titleEn: Adolescent mental health — WHO fact sheet
blogPosts.excerptFr: Données mondiales tirées de la fiche OMS.
blogPosts.excerptEn: Global prevalence snippet from WHO’s adolescent mental health factsheet.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
L’OMS autorise la reproduction de ses fiches factuelles avec la mention de source indiquée sur le site.

Globally, one in seven 10-19-year-olds experiences a mental disorder, accounting for 15% of the global burden of disease in this age group.

Source : World Health Organization, Mental health of adolescents fact sheet, who.int, consulté mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
WHO highlights the population-level burden of adolescent mental disorders to argue for school- and community-based prevention.

Globally, one in seven 10-19-year-olds experiences a mental disorder, accounting for 15% of the global burden of disease in this age group.

Source: World Health Organization, Mental health of adolescents fact sheet (who.int). Accessed May 2026. Follow WHO credit lines when republishing.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Globally, one in seven 10-19-year-olds experiences a mental disorder, accounting for 15% of the global burden of disease in this age group.

Fuente: OMS, ficha sobre salud mental de los adolescentes (who.int).

blogPosts.titleFr: Pourquoi le sommeil compte pour la santé (NIH/NHLBI)
blogPosts.titleEs: Por qué importa dormir bien — NHLBI
blogPosts.excerptFr: Message de prévention cardiorespiratoire sur le sommeil.
blogPosts.excerptEn: Quoted prevention messaging from NIH’s National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
Sleep plays a vital role in good health and well-being throughout your life.

Source : National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NIH), mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Sleep interacts with cardiovascular, metabolic, and mental health outcomes; NHLBI summarizes why healthy sleep hygiene matters alongside medical care.

Sleep plays a vital role in good health and well-being throughout your life.

Source: NHLBI — Why Is Sleep Important? Accessed May 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Sleep plays a vital role in good health and well-being throughout your life.

Fuente: NHLBI (NIH).

blogPosts.excerptFr: Définition clinique vulgarisée par le NIH.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
People may be diagnosed with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) if their symptoms last for an extended period after a traumatic event and begin to interfere with aspects of daily life, such as relationships or work.

Source : NIH/NIMH — PTSD, mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
PTSD criteria emphasize functional impairment long after the traumatic cue; early psychoeducation can normalize help-seeking.

People may be diagnosed with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) if their symptoms last for an extended period after a traumatic event and begin to interfere with aspects of daily life, such as relationships or work.

Source: NIH/NIMH — PTSD. Accessed May 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
People may be diagnosed with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) if their symptoms last for an extended period after a traumatic event and begin to interfere with aspects of daily life, such as relationships or work.

Fuente: NIMH.

blogPosts.titleFr: TDAH chez enfants et ados — NIMH
blogPosts.titleEn: ADHD in children and teens — NIMH facts
blogPosts.titleEs: TDAH en niños y adolescentes — NIMH
blogPosts.excerptFr: Début de la page grand public NIH sur le TDAH.
blogPosts.excerptEn: Leading sentence from NIH’s ADHD consumer topic page.
blogPosts.excerptEs: Frase inicial de la página del NIMH.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
ADHD is one of the most common disorders diagnosed in children.

Source : NIH/NIMH — ADHD, mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Federal descriptions highlight that ADHD begins in childhood yet often persists, which shapes treatment planning across the lifespan.

ADHD is one of the most common disorders diagnosed in children.

Source: NIH/NIMH — ADHD. Accessed May 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
ADHD is one of the most common disorders diagnosed in children.

Fuente: NIMH.

blogPosts.excerptFr: Citation courte sur l’anxiété telle que présentée aux patient·e·s par le NIH.
blogPosts.excerptEn: Quoted consumer paragraph on anxiety disorders from NIMH.
blogPosts.excerptEs: Párrafo para el público sobre ansiedad.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
Feeling anxious is a normal part of life.

Source : NIH/NIMH — Anxiety Disorders, mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Distinguishing everyday worry from disorders that merit clinical attention is a core psychoeducation topic.

Feeling anxious is a normal part of life.

Source: NIH/NIMH — Anxiety Disorders. Accessed May 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Feeling anxious is a normal part of life.

Fuente: NIMH.

blogPosts.titleFr: La dépression (trouble dépressif majeur), selon le NIMH
blogPosts.excerptFr: Définition grand public tirée du National Institute of Mental Health.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
Rappel : texte ci-dessous en anglais tel que publié par le NIH.

Everyone feels sad or low sometimes, but these feelings usually pass with time.

Source : NIH/NIMH — Depression, mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Depressive disorders are clinically serious; public-health agencies distinguish passing low mood from illnesses that need treatment.

Everyone feels sad or low sometimes, but these feelings usually pass with time.

Source: National Institute of Mental Health. Accessed May 2026. U.S. government work—not a substitute for professional assessment.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Everyone feels sad or low sometimes, but these feelings usually pass with time.

Fuente: NIMH.

blogPosts.titleEn: What counts as mental health — CDC overview
blogPosts.excerptFr: Définition grand public du Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (É.-U.).
blogPosts.excerptEn: Plain-language definition from CDC mental-health landing pages.
blogPosts.excerptEs: Defensa del bienestar mental según los CDC.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
Résumé pour lecteurs nord-américains : les CDC précisent ce que recouvre la santé mentale avant d’aborder les filières de soins.

Mental health includes a person’s emotional, psychological, and social well-being.

Source : Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Mental Health, cdc.gov/mental-health, consulté mai 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Federal public-health agencies routinely publish broad definitions before linking to crisis resources and data programs.

Mental health includes a person’s emotional, psychological, and social well-being.

Source: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (cdc.gov/mental-health). Accessed May 2026.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Mental health includes a person’s emotional, psychological, and social well-being.

Fuente: CDC (cdc.gov), mayo 2026.

blogPosts.titleFr: Trouble affectif saisonnier — rappels du NIMH
blogPosts.excerptFr: Résumé pédagogique à partir du National Institute of Mental Health (USA). Sans avis médical.
blogPosts.excerptEn: Lay summary sourced from NIH/NIMH (U.S.). Not medical advice.
blogPosts.excerptEs: Resumen educativo basado en NIMH (EE. UU.). No es consejo médico.
blogPosts.bodyFr:
Résumé éducatif pour la démo PsyNova. La citation ci-dessous provient des contenus pour le grand public du NIMH.

SAD is a type of depression characterized by a recurrent seasonal pattern, with symptoms lasting about 4–5 months out of the year.

Source : National Institute of Mental Health (NIH) — Consulté mai 2026. Œuvres du gouvernement fédéral des États-Unis.
blogPosts.bodyEn:
Educational summary for PsyNova’s public blog. Below, a verbatim excerpt drawn from NIH/NIMH consumer information (U.S. government work).

SAD is a type of depression characterized by a recurrent seasonal pattern, with symptoms lasting about 4–5 months out of the year.

Source: National Institute of Mental Health (NIH). Accessed May 2026. U.S. government work.

This page is informational only and does not replace care from a qualified clinician.
blogPosts.bodyEs:
Resumen educativo. La cita siguiente proviene del material para el público del NIMH (inglés original).

SAD is a type of depression characterized by a recurrent seasonal pattern, with symptoms lasting about 4–5 months out of the year.

Fuente: NIMH (NIH), consultado en mayo de 2026.
